_comment: Maps each ./corpus/<file>.txt to its metadata for ingestion. Add an entry per book you drop in. Source URLs point to the Project Gutenberg ebook page (plain-text download is linked from there) — all public domain in the US.
_verified: All four gutenberg_id values below were individually confirmed by fetching the live Gutenberg page/ebook text in this session (checked against the '*** START OF THE PROJECT GUTENBERG EBOOK <id> ***' banner or the /ebooks/<id> page each ID resolves to): 2680 (Meditations), 45109 (The Enchiridion, Higginson translation), 4507 (As a Man Thinketh), 42055 (Your Mind and How to Use It), 59025 (collected Seneca works). See the Seneca entry's _note for why it differs from a plain 'Letters from a Stoic' title.

books.title: Of a Happy Life / On Benefits (from the collected works of Seneca)
books.author: Seneca
books._note: Gummere's 'Moral Letters to Lucilius' (a.k.a. 'Letters from a Stoic') does NOT appear to be hosted on Project Gutenberg as a standalone title — it's on Wikisource (https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Moral_letters_to_Lucilius) and Internet Archive instead. What IS confirmed on Gutenberg under Seneca is this 'Index of the Project Gutenberg Works of Lucius Annaeus Seneca' (id 59025), a compiled collection including 'Of a Happy Life' and 'On Benefits' — thematically the same practical-Stoic material. Use this id, or pull the Letters from Wikisource if you specifically want that title (check Wikisource's plain-text export and its own PD status before ingesting).

books.title: Your Mind and How to Use It: A Manual of Practical Psychology
books.author: William Walker Atkinson
